vitals.location: Peso da Régua, Douro Valley, Portugal

course_description.overview: Road granfondo through UNESCO Douro wine region with terraced vineyard climbs above Douro River, offering granfondo (135-162 km), mediofondo (100-112 km), and minifondo (69-71 km) routes starting and finishing in Peso da Régua.[1][2][4][5]

key_segments.name: Climb to Vale de Figueira
key_segments.description: 5.6% gradient after Granja do Tedo, part of medio and granfondo routes.[2]

key_segments.name: Climb to Tabuaço
key_segments.description: 5.6% gradient via Paredes da Beira and Espinhosa on granfondo route.[2]

key_segments.name: Tedo Valley and EN222
key_segments.description: Steep gradients through Tabuaço, Barcos, Adorigo to Douro River banks by Bagaúste dam.[2]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Demanding with continuous elevation gain, few flat sections, and steep climbs through stunning terraced landscapes; suitable for endurance cyclists.[1][2][4]

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Porto Airport (OPO), then drive ~1.5 hours to Peso da Régua; trains available from Porto to Régua.[2]
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and guesthouses in Peso da Régua and nearby Douro Valley towns; book early due to wine tourism popularity.[1]
logistics.registration_details: Online via RaceFinder or BIKESERVICE; fees 46-50€; opens January 2026; limited to 3000 participants.[1][2][5]
logistics.bike_logistics: Bike transport via airlines to Porto; local bike shops in Régua for servicing; paved roads, no special requirements.[1]
logistics.local_considerations: UNESCO wine region; hot May weather possible; respect terraced vineyards; post-event wine tasting opportunities.[2][3]

final_verdict.one_liner: Iconic Portuguese granfondo blending epic Douro climbs with notable wine country scenery.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Endurance road cyclists seeking challenging hills, stunning views, and cultural immersion in a well-organized regional event.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the unbeatable combination of ride quality, scenery, and value in Europe's premier wine region.
pro_tips: Arrive early for accommodation in peak wine season.
Combine with Douro wine tours post-race.
Opt for granfondo for full Vilar reservoir and Tabuaço ascent experience.
